name: Congressman Bill Keating
tweet: May 28th must be remembered alongside January 6th as the day that 35 U.S. Senators, representing a minority of Americans, put their personal political future ahead of the future of our democracy.

name: Congressman Bill Keating
tweet: The bipartisan majority voted in favor of a January 6th commission in fear of what further damage could be inflicted on our democracy. The minority used the un-democratic filibuster to block it in fear of losing elections.

name: Congressman Bill Keating
tweet: I, along with the majority of members of the House and Senate, on a bipartisan basis and representing the vast majority of Americans, voted AGAINST doing nothing in response to the insurrection.

name: Congressman Bill Keating
tweet: Voting to block the bipartisan 1/6 commission was the minority’s way of declaring that doing nothing is their response to keeping Capitol Police, congressional staff, and members themselves safe from future attacks.

name: Congressman Bill Keating
tweet: Voting to block the bipartisan 1/6 commission was the minority’s way of declaring that doing nothing is their response to the greatest number of first responder injuries since September 11th.

name: Congressman Bill Keating
tweet: Voting to block the bipartisan 1/6 commission was the minority’s way of declaring that doing nothing is their response to the death of a Capitol Police officer who came to work every day to protect them.

name: Congressman Bill Keating
tweet: Voting to block the bipartisan 1/6 commission was the minority’s way of declaring that doing nothing is their response to an attack focused on taking hostage not just the physical documents declaring the winner of the election, but the Vice President of the United States.

name: Congressman Bill Keating
tweet: Voting to block the bipartisan 1/6 commission yesterday was the minority’s way of declaring that doing nothing is their response to an attack on our democracy.

name: Congressman Bill Keating
tweet: As I read the names of the 35 Senators who blocked the bipartisan 1/6 Commission, I thought of a quote of unknown origin, often misattributed to the great Irish statesman Sir Edmund Burke: “The only thing necessary for the triumph of evil is for good people to do nothing.”  https://t.co/knQuCu0Hle
